data.narrative:
Vatn Systems is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Nelson Mills, Freddie Mills, and Geoff Manchester, currently valued at $300M post-money with $76.5M raised to date. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Ares Industries, and NODA AI at the same stage and sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, anchored to the $300M last post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implies a valuation of $600M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $150M (range: $125M–$500M), implying $150M. These divergent signals reflect material dispersion in the Defense & Security peer set, particularly the $500M valuation of Stark Defence versus $125M for NODA AI.

Triangulation across both methods yields a median valuation of $375M, with a low bound of $128M and a high bound of $690M. The current $300M valuation sits in the lower-middle portion of this range. Key uncertainties center on the appropriate stage-progression multiplier for this sector, selectivity bias within the three-company peer median (which excludes higher-valued outliers), and whether Stark Defence's $500M valuation is a reliable comparable. Additional clarity on Vatn Systems' revenue, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to peers would refine the analysis.
